applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with pure PyTorch
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to pure PyTorch implementation. Removed custom Triton kernels and used native PyTorch operations (view/transpose/contiguous for channel shuffle). torch.compile automatically fuses BN+ReLU+shuffle+add operations. Key fix: use inplace=False for ReLU to avoid gradient computation issues.

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton conv+BN+ReLU kernels
attempted_but_failed.reason: Complex indexing in group convolution difficult to implement correctly

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with Triton kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Import error: specialize_impl not found in triton.runtime.jit

notes: BREAKTHROUGH: torch.compile on pure PyTorch achieves 1.29x speedup (4.41ms vs 5.68ms)
Custom Triton kernels were error-prone and unnecessary for this problem
Key insight: torch.compile automatically fuses elementwise ops (BN+ReLU+shuffle+add) while keeping MIOpen for convolutions
Critical fix: Use inplace=False for ReLU to avoid torch.compile gradient issues
Channel shuffle via view/transpose/view is handled efficiently by torch.compile
SCORE improved from 50 (correct but slow) to 62.9 (correct and 1.29x faster)

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 produced no output, marking 4 consecutive failures. The agent is stuck without explicit code. Providing exact shell commands with complete copy-paste solution using channels_last + reduce-over

global_notes: For AMD ROCm: torch.compile(mode='default') is the recommended approach
Avoid mixing torch.compile with custom Triton kernels due to compatibility issues
Let torch.compile handle elementwise fusion; MIOpen handles convolutions efficiently
